_comment: S7 Compounding / co-channel overlap — recovery under signals that physically sum in one slot (STUDY-SPEC §6). Each part lists explicit per-signal (msg_id, freq_hz, dt_s, snr_db); the harness sums them into one waveform. Truth is logged one row PER SIGNAL so the existing matcher scores each compounded message independently.
name: Compounding / co-channel overlap
response: Decoded / not-decoded per compounded message (attribute, per-signal recovery)
description: 4 overlap families × 3 trials. Drives: per-message recovery and between-app agreement when 2–3 signals occupy the same or near-same audio frequency / time slot — the real-world pileup case that S4 (frequency-spread QRM) never exercises.
harness_note: Rendered by _render_compound: each signal is encoded independently (own seeded noise at its snr_db, matching the S4 superposition convention) and summed. One truth row is written per signal, carrying that signal's true freq/dt/snr, so the matcher's text+freq branch scores each message on its own.

parts.label: 2-stack, equal 0 dB
parts.note: Two stations exactly co-frequency, equal strength — hardest pure separation case.

parts.label: 2-stack, equal -5 dB
parts.note: Co-frequency pair near the decode floor.

parts.label: 3-stack, equal 0 dB
parts.note: Triple co-frequency pileup — three messages in one bin.

parts.label: delta 3 Hz
parts.note: Sub-bin separation (< one 6.25 Hz tone bin).

parts.label: delta 50 Hz
parts.note: Edge-to-edge — one full FT8 bandwidth apart; expected to separate cleanly.

parts.label: co-freq, dt 0.0 / 0.5 s
parts.note: Same frequency, late replier offset by 0.5 s.

parts.label: co-freq, dt 0.0 / 2.0 s
parts.note: Same frequency, 2.0 s time stagger (near sync-window edge).

parts.label: co-freq, 0 / -3 dB
parts.note: Capture effect: 3 dB stronger vs weaker, same bin.

parts.label: co-freq, 0 / -10 dB
parts.note: 10 dB capture margin — weaker likely suppressed.

parts.label: co-freq, +3 / -10 dB
parts.note: Strong dominant signal over a weak one.
